hackathons.full_name: D.E.S.I.G.N for BioE3 Challenge: Empowering Youth to Solve Critical Issues of their TIMES
hackathons.hosting_organization: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR)
hackathons.theme: Sustainable biotechnological solutions for societal challenges under the BioE3 policy framework
problem_statements: Developing innovative, sustainable, and scalable biotechnological solutions driven by young students and researchers

eligibility_criteria.summary: Open to young students and researchers in India

prizes.summary: Recognition and support for winners — specific prize details not published on official page

hackathons.full_name: Zoological Survey of India National Hackathon 2026 — Integrating Traditional Knowledge with Technology for Biodiversity Conservation
hackathons.ministry: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
hackathons.theme: Traditional knowledge combined with contemporary technological advancements for biodiversity conservation
problem_statements: 16 themes covering various Indian ecosystems — combining traditional knowledge with innovation, AI, and data sciences to document, preserve, and utilize indigenous biodiversity knowledge systems

eligibility_criteria.summary: Open to all Indian citizens — students (school, UG, PG), researchers, innovators, startups, community practitioners. No age bar. Teams of max 3 members.

hackathons.full_name: BIOPACKATHON 2026: Biodegradable Packaging Solution Challenge for Pan Masala & Gutkha Sachets
hackathons.hosting_organization: Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS), Department of Consumer Affairs (DoCA)
hackathons.ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution
hackathons.theme: Develop plastic-free, biodegradable, food-grade sachet packaging alternatives for pan masala and gutkha that meet functional requirements and are scalable for industry adoption
problem_statements: Develop prototype-ready plastic-free, biodegradable sachet packaging for pan masala and gutkha with evidence of biodegradability, food safety, functional performance and scalability

eligibility_criteria.summary: Open to all Indian individuals and entities (innovators, startups, researchers, NGOs, industry experts). Must present prototype-ready or already developed solution with supporting evidence (test reports, certifications).

hackathons.full_name: AI for Road Safety Hackathon 2026: National AI Hackathon on Road Safety by CoERS IIT Madras with MoRTH and MeitY
hackathons.hosting_organization: Centre of Excellence for Road Safety (CoERS), IIT Madras; Ministry of Road Transport and Highways (MoRTH); Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
hackathons.ministry: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways; Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
hackathons.theme: Develop AI-powered solutions for road safety including traffic law compliance, road condition monitoring, and emergency response systems
problem_statements: DriveLegal: Location-specific traffic laws, violations and fine schedules; RoadWatch: Citizen monitoring of road conditions and spending; RoadSoS: Location-based emergency services access

eligibility_criteria.summary: Open to students, engineers, and technology enthusiasts aged 15+. Individual or team participation allowed. No professional qualifications necessary.

prizes.summary: 1st Prize: ₹50,000; 2nd Prize: ₹30,000; 3rd Prize: ₹20,000. Participation certificates for all Stage 1 submitters. Internship opportunities for selected participants.

official_open_keywords_found: launched the nationwide AI for Road Safety Hackathon 2026
